#  Set a Default Compliance Template for Commitments (Stub) 
#####  Page Content in Technical Review
This is a **Draft** page and is being reviewed for technical accuracy by subject matter experts. This page is for **Internal Use Only - Not For External Distribution**. This page contains proprietary and confidential information and is the property of Procore Technologies, Inc. _[This banner will be replaced with the appropriate banner when the content is approved for publication and released to share with Procore Pay users]_
##  Background 
With Procore Pay, a Project Admin or a user with 'Admin' level permissions on the Commitments tool can set a default compliance templates to use on commitments in Procore projects.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
  * **Additional Information** :
    * Compliance templates are only available with Procore Pay.


##  Prerequisites 
  * [Create Compliance Templates for Commitments as a Company Admin](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-compliance-tab-on-subcontractor-invoices-with-procore-pay/create-templates)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the Company level **Admin** tool.
  2. Under **Tool Settings** , click **Contracts**.
  3. Click **Compliance Templates**.
  4. In the **Compliance Templates** page, locate the template to assign to a project.


## See Also
  * [What gets copied over to a new project from a project template?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-gets-copied-over-to-a-new-project-when-applying-a-project-template)
  * [Procore Pay - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
  * [Procore Pay - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
